fault_hypothesis: Lines 73-74 incorrectly remove the last element when both lists share the same final value, which breaks the requirement to preserve all duplicates. This spurious deduplication logic should not exist.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left_ids and right_ids and merged__1[-1] == left_ids[-1] == right_ids[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid inputs with duplicates; if it fires it incorrectly removes an element.
After initialization merged must be empty.
After the pop branch executes, merged has one fewer element than the full union — this branch should never execute for duplicate-preserving semantics.
The output after stride filtering should account for all merged elements without spurious removal.
